titleStmt.title: Letter from James P Farrell to the Longford Urban District Council, 8 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James P. Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from James P Farrell MP to the Longford Urban District Council on the 8 August 1916. In the letter Farrell reports to the council on financial matters.James Patrick Farrell (1865-1921) was an MP in West Cavan after the 1895 by-election and then North Longford from 1900 until 1918 general elections. Farrell was also founder, owner and editor of the Longford Leader newspaper. He also served on the Longford Urban District Council and acted as its chairman in 1916.The Longford Urban District Council was the local authority and was renamed the Longford Town Council in 2002.

correspAction.placeName: Longford Urban District Council, Co. Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Finance Committee Report I have checked balances in Bank books
with balances on other side and certify
same to be correct. The Note Collection has lodged since
last meeting Â£202.4.3. together with
Â£95 odd lodged first days of August and is
in a position to lodge to-morrow Â£30 odd â
which he collected this evening. â This
will leave the balance uncollected about
Â£11.00 which is still high. The collector is
however working hard to get in money. Signed James P. Farrell Augt. 8th. 1916
